items.description: Dead outlets, tripping breakers, flickering lights — we find the cause instead of masking the symptom.

items.description: Indoor and outdoor lighting, from a single fixture to full LED conversions that cut your bill.

items.title: EV charger installation
items.description: Home charging points sized to your panel capacity, installed with the required dedicated circuit.

items.title: Safety inspections
items.description: Insulation resistance, grounding, and RCD tests with a written report you can keep or show your insurer.

about.title: Twenty years around live wires
about.subtitle: Careful, licensed, and allergic to improvisation.
about.body: Volt Elektro is run by two licensed electricians who started out maintaining industrial installations before switching to residential work. That background shows: we test everything twice, label every circuit, and leave you a written record of what was done. Most of our work comes from people whose 'quick fix by a friend' stopped working.
about.imageAlt: The Volt Elektro team in front of their van

items.question: My breaker keeps tripping. Is that dangerous?
items.answer: It's the breaker doing its job — but the cause needs finding. It can be an overloaded circuit, a faulty appliance, or damaged wiring. Don't swap in a bigger breaker; that's how fires start. Call us and we'll diagnose it.

items.question: Do you handle small jobs, like one socket?
items.answer: Yes. Small jobs are scheduled within a few days and charged at the standard call-out rate plus materials. No job is too small if it involves electricity done wrong.

items.question: My building has old aluminium wiring. Do I need a full rewire?
items.answer: Not always. We inspect first — sometimes targeted replacement of the most stressed circuits is enough. You get an honest written assessment either way, with prices for both options.

items.question: Are you available for emergencies?
items.answer: Yes, including nights and weekends for genuine hazards: total outages, burning smells, sparking, or water near electrics. Call — don't email — and we'll talk you through making it safe until we arrive.

cta.title: Electrical problem?
